name: Cape of Good Hope Sea Route
climate: Mediterranean in the west; oceanic with strong winds; confluence of warm Agulhas and cold Benguela currents; frequent storms

neighbors.name: Southern African Development Community
neighbors.relevance: South Africa is SADC's largest economy; Cape Town and Durban ports serve the bloc

neighbors.name: African Union
neighbors.relevance: South Africa is a key AU member; Cape route serves continental trade

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: Cape route connects South Atlantic to Indian Ocean; alternative to Suez for Asia-bound shipping

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Cape route is the primary bypass when Suez is disrupted; traffic surges during Red Sea crises

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Houthi attacks and Red Sea instability directly drive rerouting around the Cape

links.description: Primary alternative route; Cape traffic surges when Suez is blocked or Red Sea is unsafe
Red Sea security crises force rerouting via Cape, adding 10-14 days transit time
South African ports (Cape Town, Durban, Richards Bay) serve as transshipment hubs for SADC
Persian Gulf oil tankers use Cape route when avoiding Red Sea/Suez
Cape route connects BRICS members Brazil, South Africa, India, and China
Ships avoiding Suez transit the Cape instead of entering the Mediterranean via Gibraltar
